title: The End of Code Review: Coding Agents Supersede Human Inspection

research_equation.problem: Mandatory human code review imposes 10-15% overhead, 24h+ latency, and social costs, yet its defect-detection value is questionable in the era of capable coding agents.
research_equation.proposed_solution: Replace mandatory human code review with agent-driven verification, reserving human oversight for high-risk changes only.
research_equation.key_evidence: SWE-bench trajectory (2% → 70%+), automated review comparisons, structural advantage analysis
assumptions: Agent capability continues to improve at current trajectory
The stated goals of code review (Bacchelli & Bird) are the correct framing
Enterprise integration friction can be overcome
Ensemble review + calibrated uncertainty adequately mitigates hallucination risk

tri_pass_notes.first_pass_summary: A position paper arguing that coding agents have crossed a capability threshold making traditional human code review redundant. Three claims: (1) agents satisfy all review goals at lower cost; (2) agents-write-humans-review is a dead end; (3) cost-benefit has flipped. Addresses five counter-arguments including hallucination, security, prompt injection, architecture coherence, and ethical accountability.
tri_pass_notes.second_pass_methodology: Argument synthesis paper — no new empirical data. Cites established SE research (Bacchelli & Bird, Sadowski, Czerwonka) and recent AI capability evidence (SWE-bench, CodeReviewer, Copilot studies). Structured as formal argument with explicit counter-argument section.
third_pass_critique: Knowledge transfer claim is weakest — Agent-generated 'explanations' are qualitatively different from human dialogue
Czerwonka's 'reviews don't find bugs' is from 2015 — may not generalize to current practices
No discussion of team size / project scale / domain differences
SWE-bench results may not generalize to enterprise codebases with legacy complexity
Legal/regulatory constraints in finance, healthcare, aviation are not addressed

challenge_module_map.challenge: Agent hallucination and false negatives in review
challenge_module_map.proposed_module: Ensemble review with calibrated uncertainty reporting

challenge_module_map.challenge: Correlated blind spots in generation and review models
Prompt injection as new attack surface
challenge_module_map.proposed_module: Adversarial input detection + input sanitization for agent reviewers

challenge_module_map.challenge: Review capacity becoming bottleneck despite agent usage
challenge_module_map.proposed_module: Autonomous agent review gates in CI/CD pipeline

challenge_module_map.challenge: Knowledge transfer loss through reduced human interaction
challenge_module_map.proposed_module: Agent-generated on-demand architectural explanations + structured mentorship programs
